# AI recommendation — for parent reading only

*This file is intentionally kept out of the [`README.md`](./README.md) read flow so that the joint review with Jack stays grounded in the [neutral 3-way framework](./README.md#which-fits-which-priority) rather than a pre-baked answer. Use this as a parent-side reference / decision-pressure-test, not as something to walk Jack through.*

*Snapshot date: 2026-04-24. Generated by **Claude Opus 4.7** (Cursor IDE agent) synthesizing the prior research conversation across `README.md`, `hobart.md`, `hobart-plan.md`, `hobart-syracuse.md`, `hobart-questions.md`, `ap-credits.md`, `wpi-plan.md`, `background.md`, and `military.md`. Revisit at the [milestones in `hobart-questions.md`](./hobart-questions.md#revisit-dates) — particularly fall Y1 grade check and the Feb 1 Y2 Dartmouth deadline.*

---

## Headline

**Recommendation: matriculate at Hobart and run the Dartmouth-first portfolio strategy.**

Across everything we've assembled on Jack — strong AP profile (BC, CS A + Principles, US History, Physics 1 & 2 all 5s, Physics C Mech/E&M assumed 5/5), McCallie prep-school academic preparation, lightweight rower who's stated *competitive rowing culture* as a priority equal to engineering, CA resident with Cal State BSME as a natural floor, parent doing portfolio-style risk management — Hobart is the path that **maximizes optionality without forcing an early trade between his two stated priorities**. It is the only starting school where 4 years of IRA-system racing and an ABET-engineering credential are simultaneously reachable.

---

## Concrete sequencing

1. **Matriculate at Hobart Y1.** Use the [path-agnostic Y1–Y2 core in `hobart-plan.md`](./hobart-plan.md#hobart-y1y2--path-agnostic-core-used-by-2a-and-2b) — completes every partner-school STEM prereq, holds writing load to one course (the FSEM), and front-loads HWS goal courses with low-writing / easy-A picks. Hold cumulative GPA at **3.5+** through fall Y2.
2. **Apply to Dartmouth (2b) by Feb 1 Y2 — primary target.** Cheaper than Columbia (~$275K vs. ~$304K) because Y3 is a Hobart-registered exchange year that keeps the $41K/yr merit scholarship applied; lower GPA gate (~3.30 vs. 3.50); 10 months earlier than the Columbia decision so a rejection still leaves the Columbia fork open.
3. **If Dartmouth rejects, apply to Columbia (2a) by Feb 1 Y3.** Stronger Ivy brand (SEAS #21) and a cleaner rowing arc (3 Hobart IRA + 1 Columbia LW Y4, no Y4 interruption).
4. **If both Ivies reject, apply to Syracuse ECS 4+2 (2e) by senior year at the 3.2 GPA gate.** Near-automatic admit; treats the partnership as the [insurance policy it is per `hobart-syracuse.md`](./hobart-syracuse.md), not the primary target.
5. **Floor: Hobart BA + Cal State BSME (2f) at ~$242K** if everything above fails — same end-state credential as WPI at comparable cost, with 4 IRA seasons rather than 4 DIII seasons.
6. **Apply to USNA in parallel this summer as a wildcard.** Doesn't conflict with anything; preempts the whole stack if it lands.

---

## Why not the alternatives

**Why not WPI** as the primary recommendation, despite its simplicity edge: WPI's rowing is honestly mid-pack DIII regional, which doesn't satisfy *competitive rowing program with real rowing culture* on the IRA-system terms Jack has framed. The dollar savings (~$22K vs. the Hobart→Cal State floor; more vs. the Ivy paths) is real but small compared to giving up the 4 IRA seasons + 1 Ivy LW shot. WPI becomes the right answer if Jack reweights toward engineering-certainty and accepts DIII-mid-pack rowing as good enough — that's a values call he should make explicitly, not a default.

**Why not UBC** as the primary recommendation: Jack's own stated read is that LW walk-on is uncertain (~4 slots), so the realistic base case is "engineering + lifestyle, no rowing." That collapses one of his two stated priorities, which is a worse outcome on his own terms than Hobart's GPA-gate risk.

---

## Conditions that would change this recommendation

- **GPA capability uncertain.** If Jack's first-semester Hobart GPA lands below 3.0, the Ivy paths become unrealistic and the active set collapses to 2e/2f/2g — at which point WPI's 4-year ABET BS at lower cost is the better deal in retrospect. Watch fall Y1 grades closely; the [revisit dates in `hobart-questions.md`](./hobart-questions.md#revisit-dates) capture the decision points.
- **Rowing performance plateaus.** The Ivy LW boats are competitive admits even with the GPA gate cleared. If Jack isn't a credible IRA-program lightweight by end of Y2, the rowing-driven case for staying at Hobart weakens and the engineering-driven case for WPI strengthens.
- **Military service moves from "wildcard" to "aspiration."** Then 2g (Hobart BA → OCS → tuition-free MS at Dartmouth Thayer / Cornell / Stanford via GI Bill + Yellow Ribbon) becomes the dominant path on cost (~$184K) and end-state credential. Have the role-type conversation with Jack early ([`hobart-questions.md` §11.1](./hobart-questions.md#11-jack-family-conversation)).
- **Spector confirms WashU 3:3 or RPI 3:2 partnerships** ([§1.9](./hobart-questions.md#1-prof-donald-spector--hws-engineering-liaison)). Either reactivates from [`hobart-unconfirmed-paths.md`](./hobart-unconfirmed-paths.md) and adds another dual-degree fork to the portfolio — strengthens the "matriculate at Hobart" recommendation further.

---

## Unconfirmed dependencies

This recommendation rests on three P0 items still in the [`hobart-questions.md`](./hobart-questions.md) queue:

- **§3.1 — scholarship renewal terms** in the Hobart offer letter (GPA minimum, semester cap, conditions). Entire Hobart cost model assumes 3–4 years of renewal.
- **§1.1 — AP Physics C exemption** from PHYS 150/160 (not just credit). Schedule-shaping if confirmed.
- **§3.2 — Dartmouth Y3 exchange-year scholarship handling.** $41K stake on whether the merit scholarship continues during the exchange year, which is a material chunk of the 2b cost-vs-2a advantage.

Push for written confirmation on all three before fall Y1 matriculation.

---

## Why this lives outside README.md

The [`README.md`](./README.md) is the joint-review document for Jack and his parent. Surfacing a pre-baked recommendation there would anchor the conversation to a conclusion before Jack works through the trade-offs himself — the [3-way priority table](./README.md#which-fits-which-priority) is the right framing for that conversation. This file exists for the parent to use as a decision-pressure-test, sanity check, or escalation point if Jack lands on a default that doesn't fit his stated priorities.
